assertions.expectation: Checks for deprecated API usage that may have changed behavior in 1.30
assertions.reason: The response checks for deprecated API usage but for version 1.32, not 1.30 as specified in the expectation

assertions.expectation: Recommends checking admission webhooks — they may be rejecting or slowing down requests on the new version
assertions.reason: Response includes a dedicated section on admission webhook issues with commands to check webhook configurations and events

assertions.expectation: Advises checking if resource requests/limits need adjusting for any system components that changed
assertions.reason: Response mentions checking resource usage of system components and resource pressure from upgrade with kubectl top commands

assertions.expectation: Suggests examining kube-system pods for any that are crashlooping or restarting
assertions.reason: Response explicitly includes commands to check system pod status and mentions restarting problematic system pods if they're crashlooping

assertions.expectation: Includes commands to check API server metrics and latency (kubectl get --raw /metrics)
assertions.reason: Response includes the exact command 'kubectl get --raw /metrics | grep apiserver_request_total | grep deprecated' to check API server metrics

assertions.expectation: Recommends checking if NetworkPolicy or service mesh components need updating for the new version
assertions.reason: Response includes a dedicated service mesh section with Istio/ASM compatibility checks and proxy status commands

assertions.expectation: Advises reviewing pod events for scheduling delays or readiness probe failures
assertions.reason: Response includes multiple commands to check events with kubectl get events in different namespaces and for specific failure types

assertions.expectation: Mentions checking if HPA or VPA are behaving differently on the new version
assertions.reason: Response includes a dedicated section on HPA/VPA behavioral changes with commands to check HPA status and scaling events
